sources.title: Study finds many dealers fall short on key lead processes
sources.excerpt: DAS Technology coverage of the 2025 Lead Response Study notes that many dealerships still respond slowly or miss basic lead-process steps.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the article's broader context that response gaps and follow-up inconsistency remain a dealership operating problem.

sources.title: 2025 Automotive Dealer Benchmarks Report
sources.excerpt: Foureyes analyzes dealership website and lead-process data, including CRM logging issues, mishandled leads, and follow-up timing.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the article's argument that CRM discipline and follow-up consistency create measurable leakage in dealership sales processes.

sources.title: New Study: How Dealership Communication Drives Financial Success
sources.excerpt: CDK Global frames communication, KPI visibility, and cross-rooftop consistency as drivers of dealership financial performance.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the article's multi-rooftop operating angle around shared standards, consistent communication, and group-level visibility.

sources.title: The Short Life of Online Sales Leads
sources.excerpt: Harvard Business Review summarizes research showing that many companies do not respond quickly enough to online sales inquiries.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the speed-to-lead context behind the case study's instant-response operating layer.
sources.freshness_override_reason: Older than the default 24-month window, but used as evergreen foundational response-speed research rather than a current market-size claim.

sources.notes: Four public contextual sources are listed and linked in the article. The proprietary revenue and contact figures are identified as VisQuanta internal conversion-tracking data.

competitor.notes: No blocked competitor names or competitor links appear in the publish output.

content_quality.notes: The article stays focused on the Oklahoma five-rooftop operating story, includes old-lead reactivation detail, and avoids exposing dealer identities.

seo_aeo.notes: Clean title, descriptive meta description, case-study category, entity-rich automotive language, methodology, and FAQ are present.

links.notes: The post includes internal VisQuanta links, contextual source links, and external channel links with descriptive anchors.

calculator.notes: No calculator is required for this case study because the post reports an observed Oklahoma dealer group deployment result.

visual.notes: The article hero is hidden and the post now uses an inline rooftop revenue chart instead of the platform dashboard screenshot.

sources.notes: External sources are used only for public industry context; case-study results are internal platform-tracking figures.

draft.notes: Draft reviewed after the wording change from playbook to system.

seo_aeo.notes: FAQ, methodology, internal links, external source links, and product relevance are present.

visual.notes: Dashboard screenshot was removed from the article view and replaced with a purpose-built rooftop revenue chart.

final_publish.notes: Approved for PR merge once GitHub checks pass.
